original_prompt: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. What is my Facebook security code? It's in an email I received recently. 2. Please get me the link to reset my TechServices password. It should be in an email I received recently.
assessment_reasoning: The user specifies two concrete goals (retrieve a Facebook security code and a TechServices password-reset link) and the order to perform them, which gives clear high-level intent. However key execution details are missing: which email account or mailbox to search, what time range “recently” means, what exact output format is expected (just the code/link, or full email contents/metadata), and how to handle authentication/permissions. These gaps mean the agent must make assumptions about the environment and output format. Therefore the prompt is moderately ambiguous.

variations.L1: I need two things from my email: the Facebook security code I was sent recently, and the link to reset my TechServices password. Do that in that order.
variations.L2: Please do the following, in order: 1) Find the Facebook security code that was emailed to me recently. 2) Find the password-reset link for my TechServices account that should be in a recent email. Return what you find.
variations.L3: Perform these tasks in order: 1) Locate the Facebook security code from an email I received recently and give me the code. 2) Locate the TechServices password-reset link from an email I received recently and give me the link. Search my inbox for recent messages if needed and report the results.
variations.L4: Do these two tasks in order and be specific: 1) Search my email inbox for recent messages from Facebook (e.g., sender addresses like @facebookmail.com or subjects containing "Facebook" or "security code") and extract the security code that was sent to me. Provide the code, the email subject, sender, and date/time. 2) Then search my inbox for recent TechServices password-reset emails (subjects containing "reset" or "password" or from the TechServices sender) and extract the password reset link. Provide the link, the email subject, sender, and date/time. If you can't find either item, say which step failed and why.
variations.L5: Follow these exact steps, in order, using my email account: 1) Use the mail search API to run the query (within the last 7 days) for Facebook messages, e.g. subject:(Facebook OR "security code") OR from:(@facebookmail.com OR security@facebook.com). Open the most recent matching email, extract the Facebook security code (typically a 5–8 digit numeric code), and return only the code plus metadata: {"facebook_code": "<code>", "email_subject": "<subject>", "email_from": "<sender>", "email_date": "<iso8601>"}. Do not print the full email body. 2) Then run the mail search for TechServices password-reset messages, e.g. subject:(TechServices OR "password reset" OR "reset your password") OR from:(*@techservices.example.com), open the most recent matching email, extract the password-reset URL (the first http(s) link in the message), verify the link responds (HTTP HEAD) and return: {"techservices_reset_url": "<url>", "email_subject": "<subject>", "email_from": "<sender>", "email_date": "<iso8601>", "url_valid": true/false}. If any item is not found, return a clear error entry for that step. Execute exactly in this order and do not perform any other actions.
